judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports correctly identify all three root causes and fixes with equivalent depth and accuracy; Report 1 adds extra (optional) detail like FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY and SET LOCAL, while Report 2 adds an optional startup-assertion suggestion — neither extra is required by the expectations, making the two reports comparable in coverage.

report_1.evidence: the owner of a table always bypasses row-level security policies unless the table has FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY explicitly set — and 0001_init.sql never sets FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on either table... cedarline/.env configures the running FastAPI app's own DATABASE_URL to also be cedar_owner, not the dedicated cedar_app role

report_2.evidence: DATABASE_URL is set to postgresql://cedar_owner:ownerpw@localhost:5432/cedar — the schema-owning Postgres role that ran the migrations... In Postgres, a table owner (and any role with BYPASSRLS, which table owners effectively have with respect to their own tables) is exempt from row-level security policies by default, regardless of any SET app.current_tenant = ... executed per-request in deps.py

report_1.evidence: Change the application's DATABASE_URL (in cedarline/.env and .env.example, and whatever env cedarline runs on) to connect as cedar_app, not cedar_owner. The app should never hold the owning/migration credentials at runtime... Keep migrations running as the owning role (cedar_owner) — that separation (owner runs DDL, unprivileged app role runs DML under RLS) is the right model

report_2.evidence: Change cedarline/.env (and .env.example, and any deployed equivalent) so the running application's DATABASE_URL uses the least-privilege cedar_app role/password... cedar_owner: used only by scripts/migrate.sh / migration tooling, never referenced by the running backend. cedar_app: the only role the FastAPI app (app/db.py, app/deps.py) ever connects with

report_1.evidence: The role cedar_app is created solely by db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql, which per its own comment and docs/local-setup.md/README.md is a "one-time... run by hand" step, and it is not invoked by reset-local-db.sh. So after a wipe+rebuild, cedar_app no longer exists in the fresh cluster... the teammate needs to manually re-run psql "postgresql://cedar_owner:ownerpw@localhost:5432/cedar" -f db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql after every wipe

report_2.evidence: It never re-runs cedarline/db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql. That bootstrap file's own header states it is "Not part of automatic database initialization; run by hand on first setup," and docs/local-setup.md confirms creating cedar_app is a separate manual step... To fix it, someone must manually re-run psql "postgresql://cedar_owner:ownerpw@localhost:5432/cedar" -f db/bootstrap/create_app_role.sql after every reset
